mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in residential demand, which amplifies financial sector stress and reduces consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or housing metrics decouple from equity performance during financial stress.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and falling corporate revenues, which triggers sharp negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week only during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive or flat 5-day forward returns during tightening regimes, or the predictive relationship reverses sign when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and stronger corporate earnings expectations, which systematically support higher equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 70% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 210.0, or inflation thresholds fail to predict direction with a t-statistic greater than 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real sales of existing homes, where GDP expands while housing sales contract, signals structural demand imbalances that eventually drag on broader economic activity and yield negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS changes shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or the divergence pattern correlates positively with equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in the federal funds rate indicates a policy response to labor market deterioration, which confirms recession onset and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during credit crunch conditions.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns during credit stress periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
